Off Calendar Feb-05-2014 Continued To May-05-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust Amendment And For Order Determining Interest In Property, Supporting Declaration Of Robert J. Sheppard, And Supporting Memorandum
Off Calendar Nov-20-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian McCaffrey for Charles Aguilina. Julianna Pisani for Rocseline Aquilina (Respondent/Benficiary).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the 120 day notice requirement, the Trustee not giving himself notice to not trigger the 120 day statute, the Trustee knowing the terms of the Trust, the duties of the Trustee, etc. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court noted that neither Party addressed the motion as it relates to the order determining interest in property. The Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Respondents' Motion to Dismiss Petition to Determine Validity of Purported Trust Amendment is granted. Parties failed to address the Order Determining Interest in Property. The motion as it relates to the Order Determining Interest in Property is denied without prejudice (order for the same signed in open court). Mr. McCaffrey approved the order as to form.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)
